About me

I look closely at
the things hiding
in plain sight.

UX specialist with a background in systematic design and HCI. I help teams understand people — not just users — and turn that understanding into products that actually fit. Currently pursuing an MSc at DTU while working at the crossroads of research, strategy, and craft.

More about me ⬇ Download CV
Experience

Where I've worked

Designing evaluation strategies and tools for skills development initiatives. Facilitating co-creation workshops with stakeholders to identify gaps and needs. Analysing data and engagement metrics from learning programs, and mapping technology and skills for project scope and development.

Evaluation DesignWorkshop FacilitationData AnalysisSkills Mapping

Directed research strategy to align doctor and patient requirements at the UX and service levels. Standardised research frameworks for cross-team alignment. Scaled research maturity through in-house training, systematised the research database to map healthcare requirements, and optimised recruitment and ops for usability testing on medical platforms.

Research StrategyResearchOpsUsability TestingHealthTech

Established scalable research systems and best practices for growth. Led UX design for onboarding and purchase flows from research to logic. Created wireframes and prototypes for iterative user validation, partnered with UI and dev teams on logic-heavy features, planned and conducted global usability tests, defined UX KPIs and dashboards, built a research repository, and translated research into technical hand-off specifications.

Onboarding DesignUX KPIsWireframingGlobal Testing

Conducted field and contextual research to map complex outdoor user behaviours. Co-created interactive science-learning systems via stakeholder workshops. Designed UX flows and wrote all in-app UX copy for the mobile hiking app. Synthesised complex scientific topics into user-friendly content, ran field user tests, and partnered with developers for system optimisations.

About me

I design for the space between intent and understanding.

I'm a UX specialist based in Copenhagen, currently pursuing an MSc in Design & Innovation at DTU. With 4+ years bridging user research and product strategy in B2B SaaS, I help teams see the gap between what people say and what they actually need — and build toward the latter.

My background spans healthcare, EdTech, science communication, and digital products. I've always been drawn to the complex and the underexplored — the kind of problems where curiosity is the sharpest tool in the kit.

"By being curious, we open ourselves up to the potential value of the things that would otherwise pass us by — this is the realm of beauty."

I think of design as a practice of paying attention. Looking closely at what people do, not just what they say. Finding the edges where the expected breaks down and something more interesting begins.

⬇ Download CV Get in touch
Based in
Copenhagen
What drives me

How I approach work

🔬
Curiosity first
I don't start with a solution. I start with a question — and I let the research shape where I go. Real curiosity means being surprised, and being okay with having been wrong.
🌉
Bridging worlds
My best work happens at intersections — between user needs and system constraints, between research insight and product direction, between technical logic and human feeling.
🪨
Layers matter
Good design, like good geology, is about understanding what's beneath the surface. The detail you almost miss is usually the one that holds everything together.

Back to Work
3

Onboarding

for a Digital Signage Web App

Yodeck is a digital signage platform that needed to improve its user experience for new users in order to better compete in the US and global market.

The goal was to create a user-friendly onboarding experience that would help new users adopt the system and contribute to long-term business growth.

As the leader of the research for the project, I identified the needs, pain points, and successes of new users. Based on the findings, we designed in-app tours, guides, and emails to enhance the overall user experience.

Responsibilities
User Interviews Journey Mapping User Flows Wireframes UX Writing
Duration
5–6 months
Company
Yodeck (Flipnode LLC)
Digital signage SaaS · B2B Global
Year
2023–2024
Process & Outcomes

We defined the problem space, designed prototypes and implemented multiple touchpoints to improve the new user's experience.

Kick off
User Interviews
Usability Benchmarking
Design Specifications
Wireframe Design
New User Flows
Deliverables
In-App
Onboarding Library
In-App
Screen Set-Up Guides
Email
Onboarding Email Series
User Interviews

11 active new users. Real friction, real behaviour.

I interviewed 11 active new Yodeck users to dig deeper into their initial onboarding experience. As a result, we validated some hypotheses according to user behaviour and got new insights for their respective personas. I used this data to map the new user's journey and pinpoint the main friction areas.

🎨
Non-tech users were more interested in how to create content first, and then see it on a test screen.
🖥️
Tech users instantly look for how to set up a test screen — skipping content creation entirely.
⏭️
Users get annoyed by the onboarding tour and prefer to skip it to explore the app themselves.
🔁
Users have to take a repetitive journey to upload and create content — the flow didn't remember context.
😕
Non-tech users get confused when trying to understand how to test on a screen what they have created.
🤝
Users need guidance from a human to learn how to work the features — self-serve alone wasn't enough.
UX Survey

Measuring what "usable" actually meant to users.

There were 2 questions about the current UX of the onboarding tour that we wanted to answer:

  • How the onboarding tour meets their needs
  • How the onboarding functionalities are easy to use

I launched an in-app survey for new users using the standardised UMUX-Lite framework (Usability Metric for User Experience). The score was later converted into the industry standard SUS score (System Usability Scale).

97 responses were collected, producing a final Usefulness Score compared with company KPIs such as NPS.

47–61
Out of 100
Usefulness Score (SUS)
Utility: 4.9  ·  Usability: 5.1
Score achieved47–61 / 100
PoorMarginalOKGoodExcellent

The current onboarding tools were perceived as "Poor, Not acceptable" to "OK, Marginal" by new users — well below acceptable thresholds.

Flow & Wireframe Design

From insight to logic — redesigning what came before.

Based on the research findings, we worked together with the UI designer to ideate new solutions. I designed new optimised user flows, and created wireframes that included new sub-features.

New User Flow — Onboarding redesign
Non-tech path
Sign Up
→
Goal selection
→
Create content
→
Preview on demo screen
→
Connect real screen
Tech path
Sign Up
→
Goal selection
→
Connect real screen
→
Add content
→
Go live
Onboarding wireframe — goal-based routing step

Key design principle: show value before asking for effort. The old flow front-loaded configuration. The new flow let users see Yodeck working — with a demo screen — before they'd set up anything themselves.

Additional Solutions

Beyond the app — a multi-touchpoint onboarding system.

In addition to the in-app onboarding, we created quick screen set-up guides for test screens. We collaborated with the marketing and engineering teams to create and programme a new email series to assist users with learning the basic features, and increase adoption.

📱
In-App Onboarding Library
Contextual tooltips, progressive checklists, and in-app guides that activated based on user behaviour — not time-based triggers.
🖥️
Screen Set-Up Guides
Step-by-step illustrated guides for setting up a test screen — built for non-tech users who needed hardware setup support alongside the software.
✉️
Onboarding Email Series
A sequenced email series written to meet users where they were — different paths for tech vs non-tech users, timed to key behavioural triggers.
Outcome

A smoother path to value — for users and the business.

The implementation of the new onboarding system streamlined the user experience, resulting in a smoother and quicker onboarding process for new users. This translated into a quicker time-to-value for both users and the business, as users were able to quickly understand and utilise the platform's features.

⚡
Quicker time-to-value
Users reached their first meaningful screen setup faster — both the tech and non-tech paths were shortened significantly.
🎫
Fewer support tickets
Relevant support tickets decreased as the new guides and email series resolved the most common friction points proactively.
😊
Improved user satisfaction
Overall user satisfaction improved — and the UMUX-Lite score was used as an ongoing benchmark for future onboarding iterations.

This project also optimised resources — by reducing repetitive support queries, the team could redirect effort toward product development rather than reactive troubleshooting.
